articles.title: Pakistan: Air fares likely to increase after government raises jet fuel prices
articles.description: Airfares in Pakistan are likely to increase after the government raised the price of jet fuel by PKR 13.23 per litre, following a recent hike in petrol prices, ARY News reported.

articles.title: Tensions Rise as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Amidst Escalating Conflict
articles.description: Iran has closed the crucial Strait of Hormuz after a vessel violated route protocols, warning of a severe response to any retaliation. The incident exacerbates tensions with the U.S., with potential impacts on global oil prices and ongoing negotiations among regional players to de-escalate the situation.

articles.title: Kejriwal visits Delhi petrol pump, service station to hear consumers' E20 fuel concerns
articles.description: NEW DELHI, Jul 11: AAP national convenor Arvind Kejriwal on Saturday visited a petrol pump and service station in Delhi to interact with vehicle owners over their experience with E20-blended petrol, claiming that several consumers complained of reduced mileage and...

articles.title: Iran rejects US talks as Hormuz shipping slows
articles.description: Iran has refused to restart talks with the US unless Washington retreats from its current positions, specifically demanding the restoration of oil exports and control over Hormuz shipping arrangements. This follows US strikes on 90 Iranian military targets in response to attacks on commercial vessels, which have significantly reduced traffic through the Strait. Diplomatic efforts involving Oman and Qatar are underway, while the IMO has rejected Iran's sovereignty claims over the waterway. The tensions continue to impact global oil prices, with the IMF warning of prolonged inflation risks.

articles.title: After ‘spot check’, Kejriwal takes aim at govt.’s ‘E20 lies’
articles.description: Kejriwal criticizes the government for misleading claims about E20 petrol's negative impact on vehicle mileage and performance.

articles.title: Petrol stations caught breaking 'mandatory' new rule with drivers warned
articles.description: New research shows petrol stations are failing to act, despite it being mandatory, and the risk of businesses paying hefty fines.

articles.title: Can Australia avoid the next round of the global energy crisis?
articles.description: As the conflict has dragged on, gas prices have soared and now threaten to up-end the economies of Europe and Asia.

articles.title: UK drivers at risk of petrol and diesel ‘price spikes’ in new fuel warning
articles.description: Petrol and diesel owners have already been hit with a massive rise at the petrol pumps, and things could still get a lot worse.

articles.title: Bounce May Fade in Oil Futures
articles.description: Oil prices recovered briefly, but bearish trends suggest traders should consider selling crude futures.
